name: My Hero Academia | [v3.1]
description:
(LAST UPDATED: May 19 2026)

This bot has most of the relevant information from the My Hero Academia Wiki (through Lorebooks). It also accounts for the logistic of having a 21st student in 1-A.

This bot was originally created with the scenario of a new student transferring to U.A., but I have improved it since then, and it's now a general My Hero Academia bot — however, the lorebooks still account for the logistics of having additional students in the class (like a 21st student in Class 1-A).

Note that the story is supposed to be during the start of the first year in U.A., and I haven't seen the bot get it wrong yet, but I'm imagining it might.

Please leave a comment if something is set wrong, like someone's appearance, personality or quirk. I will take a look at feedback every now and then. Any suggestions are also going to be appreciated.


It includes:

• Context — General world context; UA context;

• Students and Teachers — All of 1-A (students and teachers); All of 1-B (students and teachers); Other relevant students (Big Three, Mei, Mawata, Yuyu);

• UA Personel — Relevant employees (Recovery Girl, Thirteen, Hound Dog);

• Heroes — Top 10 ranked heroes (All Might is still no.1 — I'm following the list from the start of the series); Other relevant heroes (Ryukyu, Fat Gum, Mt. Lady);

• Villains — All For One; Most of the League of Villains; Other relevant villains (Stain, Overhaul, Gentle Criminal, Nine, Giren, Garaki, Nomu types);

• Statistics — Villain Points and Rescue Points 1-A students did at the entrance exam, where the user is undefined (which means you can define that through the character's memory too, if you find it relevant);

• Attributes — Power (physical strength, endurance, and destructive power), Speed (physical speed and reaction time), Technique (maestry with weapons, use of items and quirk), Intellect (prediction and thinking skills, as well as emotional control, strategy and instincts), Support (teamwork and the capability of aiding others); ranked from F (completely inept) to S+ (complete domain). Which means it should be able to deal with combat nicely, especially if you also define these attributes for your persona - you can do it through the character's memory. I usually do something like:


name's Attributes:
- Power=A
- Speed=B+
- Technique=C
- Intellect=D-
- Support=E


Scenarios:

1) New student arriving at the dormitory room in a weekend.

2) New student arriving at the classroom in a w
